rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELSIE ANN LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The affidavit filed by Audry Lee on 11/5/01 appears to have transferred a 100% interest in 2775 Crest Road, Atwater to him, even though there were other intestate heirs. 2) No estate has been opened for Barbara Lee. Petitioners allege that a personal representative for her estate is not required as Schedule A of Barbara Lee's trust "declares [her] interest in 279 Arch Street" and "[a]ccordingly, no probate administration or Heggstad petition is necessary to confer[her] interest in this Estate to her Trust". 3) Attorney alleges that they have a fee agreement with the petitioners for their personal payment of attorney fees. They request approval of their fees, even though the estate will not pay them, as Probate Code 10813 disallows fees in excess of statutory fees. Timesheets for ordinary and extraordinary services are attached to the declaration filed on 12/29/23; however, the descriptions for the extraordinary services do not adequately show that they extraordinary in nature (e.g. "status/file review"). 4) The omnibus clause requests distribution of Barbara Lee's interest of after-discovered assets to the Barbara Lee Trust, however: a) Schedule A of Barbara Lee's trust only references 279 Arch; and b) Jason Duty assigned "the entirety of the Trust's interest in the Decedent's Estate" to Audry Lee. The 11/14/23 declaration attaches an "amended" assignment, which now only assigns 279 Arch to Audry Lee; however, no revocation of the previous assignment has been filed, and no authority has been provided to show that the assignment is revocable. In addition, Petitioner has not addressed how Barbara Lee's trust has any interest in any asset in estate, other than possibly 279 Arch. 5) The omnibus clause requests distribution of Anita Duty's interest in after-discovered assets to herself, however, Anita Duty assigned "the entirety of the Trust's interest in the Decedent's Estate" to Audry Lee. The 11/14/23 declaration attaches an "amended" assignment, which now only assigns 279 Arch to Audry Lee; however, no revocation of the previous assignment has been filed, and no authority has been provided to show that the assignment is revocable. 6) Attorney requests reimbursement for costs of filing three reports of status of administration; however, only two have been filed. Attorney's 12/29/23 declaration states that their 2/3/22 entry for $200 described as "statutory fee" was misidentified and should have been labelled as "first Status Report"; however, there is already an entry on 1/5/22 for the same. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDUARDA O. BUTAC
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/24/2024, as the following issues (see tentative rulings for 02/26/2024 & 03/18/2024 hearings) remain unaddressed: 1) Notice must be given to the successors of the postdeceased beneficiaries who have not signed a power of attorney authorizing Kemp & Associates to receive notice, as the powers of attorney executed by the postdeceased beneficiaries are no longer effective. 2) An original small estate affidavit (13100 declaration) signed by all the successors of Azuceno Olorvida must be filed with a certified copy of his death certificate. The affidavit must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). LRSF 14.59(C)(2). 3) The statements of the Chase, US Bank, and Bank of America accounts must cover periods through and including 06/30/2023, the ending date of the account. Also, the attorney's declaration filed on 03/08/2024 did not include language required by CCP section 2015.5 (either a place of execution within California or "under the laws of the State of California" must be stated). 4) No prior court approval has been obtained to retain the real estate attorney and make payment from the estate. 5) Are the services performed by California Inheritance Group ordinary services that should have been performed by the administrator? If so, should CIG's 10% fee paid from the estate be deducted from the administrator's statutory compensation? 6) The schedule of proposed distribution must include a specific amount of net cash distributable to each distributee, assignee, and alternate distributee required to be identified by Probate Code section 11603(c). The proposed order must include the same descriptions. 7) A review of a copy of the closing escrow statement attached to the supplement filed on 03/05/2024 shows the total costs of $76,734.85, but Schedule E attached to the petition shows $77.535.21. 8) There is only one witness to the powers of attorney purportedly signed by Edlen Alayon. Direct notice must be given to him or her if the power of attorney is invalid. 9) There are no signatures of the witnesses to the power of attorney purportedly signed by Victor Beltran. Direct notice must be given to him if the power of attorney is invalid. 10) The power of attorney purportedly signed by Arnold Olorvida with his fingerprint was not dated. Direct notice must be given to him if the power of attorney is invalid. 11) There are no assignments signed by Salome Olorvida, Cristy Amoro, Allan A.S. Olorvida, Daniel Olorvida, Gliza Olorvida, Franz Olorvida, Artemio loysaynon, Agapito Losaynon, and Artemio Losaynon. Note: No reserve for closing expenses has been requested.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/20/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Both Supplement Two and Supplement Three AMENDED the petition and/or the accounting and the proposed distribution amounts, including a request now to withhold $110,000. (2) The amended accounting attached to Supplement Three still does not comport at all with Probate Code Section 1062. (3) There are also now "unaccounted receipts." Unsupported receipts cannot be included in the fee base. (4) Schedule 5, showing $57,438.25 total disbursements, is not verified by the seller's closing statement, which shows total costs of $57,232.00. (5) If the heirs or successors-in-interest of post-deceased heirs will not waive an accounting, a proper, complete accounting must be filed. (6) The statutory fee requested is incorrect. (7) Even if accounting is waived, detailed schedules must be included if the statutory fee is calculated on any amount other than the I&A. (8) Notice cannot be served in care of another person, unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed that notice be given to that other person. If an interested party is represented by an attorney, notice must ALSO be given to the attorney, but not only to the attorney. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b) and Probate Code Section 1214.] An amendment to the petition must be filed to address these issues. After the amendment is e-filed, notice of the amendment must be served. Clear and complete (not cut off or blurry) courtesy copies must be received by the assigned examiner by 5/10/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The attorney never completed the Statement About the Bond on either I&A.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSE CHIHHSI CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Set One From Respondent Andrew Chen Aka Andy Chen; Notice Of Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,185.00 Against Respondent Andrew Chen Aka Andy Chen
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 5 - Andy Chen] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Petitioner Chun Yen Chen's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories - Set One from Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen; Notice of Request for Monetary Sanctions in the Amount of $2,185.00 Against Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen. Background: On December 4, 2023, Petitioner propounded a set of Request for Admissions, Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories and Request for Production of Documents - Set One on Respondent Mikki Chen. Reponses to the discovery were due by January 8, 2024, per statute. No response was served by January 8, 2024. Per the Opposition, "Respondents requested a 30-day extension on December 20, 2023" on the January 8, 2024, deadline to respond to the propounded discovery (Opp. at 4:14-19). This request was not granted (see, Ex. B to Opp.). The January 8, 2024, deadline passed without Respondents serving any response to the propounded discovery. On January 16, 2024, Petitioner's attorney emailed Andy Chen and extended the deadline to respond to January 23, 2024. The email advised Respondents that all objections had been waived per statute, and cautioned that Petitioner would bring a motion to compel if Respondents did not serve responses without objections by the January 23, 2024, deadline (Decl. of Yang, Ex. 2). On January 23, 2024, Mikki Chen and Andy Chen jointly served only objections to the Form Interrogatories (Decl. of Yang, Ex. 3). On the same day, Respondents also served responses to the propounded Request for Admissions, Special Interrogatories and Request for Production of Documents, which are not at issue in this Motion. Petitioner's attorney and Andy Chen thereafter engaged in written meet and confer communications, over email, regarding Respondents' concerns and questions about responding to the Form Interrogatories and a further-extended deadline for responses without objections - ultimately February 23, 2024 (see, Decl. of Yang, Exs. 4, 5, 6, 7, 8; and Opp. Exs. A, B, D). On February 23, 2024, Mikki Chen served Supplemental Responses to the Form Interrogatories (Decl. of Yang, Ex. 9). There were joint responses to Form Interrogatory 1.1, 2.9, 2.10, 50.1, 50.3, in improper format under Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.210. Otherwise, the supplemental responses to Form Interrogatories 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.11, 2.12, 2.13, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.6, 12.7, 13.1, 13.2, 14.1, 14.2, 50.2, 50.4, 50.5, 50.6 were still only objections, without substantive responses. This Motion was filed March 11, 2024.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSE CHIHHSI CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Set One From Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen; Notice Of Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,185.00 Against Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 5 - Mikki Chen] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Petitioner Chun Yen Chen's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories - Set One from Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen; Notice of Request for Monetary Sanctions in the Amount of $2,185.00 Against Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen. Background: On December 4, 2023, Petitioner propounded a set of Request for Admissions, Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories and Request for Production of Documents - Set One on Respondent Mikki Chen. Reponses to the discovery were due by January 8, 2024, per statute. No response was served by January 8, 2024. Per the Opposition, "Respondents requested a 30-day extension on December 20, 2023" on the January 8, 2024, deadline to respond to the propounded discovery (Opp. at 4:14-19). This request was not granted (see, Ex. B to Opp.). The January 8, 2024, deadline passed without Respondents serving any response to the propounded discovery. On January 16, 2024, Petitioner's attorney emailed Andy Chen and extended the deadline to respond to January 23, 2024. The email advised Respondents that all objections had been waived per statute, and cautioned that Petitioner would bring a motion to compel if Respondents did not serve responses without objections by the January 23, 2024, deadline (Decl. of Yang, Ex. 2). On January 23, 2024, Mikki Chen and Andy Chen jointly served only objections to the Form Interrogatories (Decl. of Yang, Ex. 3). On the same day, Respondents also served responses to the propounded Request for Admissions, Special Interrogatories and Request for Production of Documents, which are not at issue in this Motion. Petitioner's attorney and Andy Chen thereafter engaged in written meet and confer communications, over email, regarding Respondents' concerns and questions about responding to the Form Interrogatories and a further-extended deadline for responses without objections - ultimately February 23, 2024 (see, Decl. of Yang, Exs. 4, 5, 6, 7, 8; and Opp. Exs. A, B, D). On February 23, 2024, Mikki Chen served Supplemental Responses to the Form Interrogatories (Decl. of Yang, Ex. 9). There were joint responses to Form Interrogatory 1.1, 2.9, 2.10, 50.1, 50.3, in improper format under Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.210. Otherwise, the supplemental responses to Form Interrogatories 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.11, 2.12, 2.13, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.6, 12.7, 13.1, 13.2, 14.1, 14.2, 50.2, 50.4, 50.5, 50.6 were still only objections, without substantive responses. This Motion was filed March 11, 2024.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSE CHIHHSI CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Set One From Respondent Andrew Chen Aka Andy Chen; Notice Of Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,185.00 Against Respondent Andrew Chen Aka Andy Chen (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 5 - Andy Chen] Discussion: Unless otherwise limited by order of the court in accordance with this title, any party may obtain discovery regarding any matter, not privileged, that is relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action [ ], if the matter either is itself admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Discovery may relate to the claim or defense of the party seeking discovery or of any other party to the action. Discovery may be obtained of the identity and location of persons having knowledge of any discoverable matter, as well as of the existence, description, nature, custody, condition, and location of any document, electronically stored information, tangible thing, or land or other property. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010) Any party may obtain discovery by one or more of the following methods: (a) Oral and written depositions. (b) Interrogatories to a party. (c) Inspections of documents, things, and places. (d) Physical and mental examinations. (e) Requests for admissions. (f) Simultaneous exchanges of expert trial witness information. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2019.010, emphasis added.) Subject to certain statutory restrictions (Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010 et seq and Code of Civil Procedure section 2019.010 et seq), any party may obtain discovery by propounding to any other party to the action written interrogatories to be answered under oath. An interrogatory may relate to whether another party is making a certain contention, or to the facts, witnesses, and writings on which a contention is based. An interrogatory is not objectionable because an answer to it involves an opinion or contention that relates to fact or the application of law to fact, or would be based on information obtained or legal theories developed in anticipation of litigation or in preparation for trial. (Civ. Proc. Code sec. 2030.010) The Judicial Council, the policymaking body of the California courts, has developed and approved official Form Interrogatories (Form FI-120). Form interrogatories can be used in a civil action that is based on a breach of contract claim. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.710). On October 27, 2023, Mikki May Chiu Chen filed a petition pursuant to Probate Code section 850, alleging elder abuse and claiming an ownership interest in: A condominium at 855 La Playa, #462 San Francisco, CA 94121; 'Sweet Potato Enterprises, Inc.', owner of a Popeyes franchise located at 599 Divisadero Street, San Francisco, CA 94117; Equity in stocks: 2916 shares of Tesla and 310 shares of Apple. On November 1, 2023, Chun Yen Chen filed a competing petition pursuant to Probate Code section 850, claiming the estate's ownership interests in the same assets, and seeking additional statutory damages, and civil and punitive damages. Both petitions bring claims based on alleged breaches of contract. Form interrogatories can be used by both petitioning parties in this matter. The party served with Form Interrogatories must serve responses within 30 days of service of the Form Interrogatories (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.260(a)). If a party fails to serve a timely response to the interrogatories, the party has waived any right to exercise the option to produce writings under Section 2030.230, as well as any objection to the interrogatories, including one based on privilege or on the protection for work product. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290(a)(1).)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSE CHIHHSI CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Set One From Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen; Notice Of Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,185.00 Against Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 5 - Mikki Chen] Self-represented parties must comply with statutory deadlines and are not immune from the consequences of a failure to do so. (Stover v. Bruntz (2017) 12 Cal.App.5th 19, 31, failure to comply with time limit for responding to requests for admissions, and imposition of statutory sanctions). Self-represented parties must be treated in the same manner as other litigants and attorneys, and must follow the correct rules of procedure, and all applicable statutes, Local Rules, and the California Rules of Court. (Rappleyea v Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 984-985; see also, Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 1.6(15), which defines "parties" as including both self-represented persons and persons represented by an attorney of record without making any distinction between them). Responses to the Form Interrogatories Petitioner served on December 4, 2023, were due January 8, 2024. Respondents did not serve responses to the Form Interrogatories by January 8, 2024. The record of communications between Petitioner's counsel and Andy Chen show that Petitioner extended the deadline for responses more than once after January 8, 2024 - ultimately to February 23, 2024 - but did not allow them to respond with objections or otherwise relieve Respondents of having waived any right to exercise the option to produce writings under Section 2030.230, as well as any objection to the interrogatories (see, Decl. of Yang, Exs. 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8; and Opp. Exs. A, B, D; see also, Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290(a)(1)). The Opposition argues, with no analysis, "It's important to note that CCCP [sic] sec 2030.290(a)(1) and 2030.290(a)(2) implicitly recognize the concept of substantial compliance as a valid defense against accusations of noncompliance." (Opp. 2:24-25.) Respondents misunderstand this portion of the statute. A party who fails to respond to interrogatories by the statutory deadline waives any right to exercise the option to produce writings under Section 2030.230, as well as any objection to the interrogatories (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290(a)(1)). Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290(a), "The court, on motion, may relieve that party from this waiver on its determination that both of the following conditions are satisfied: (1) The party has subsequently served a response that is in substantial compliance with Sections 2030.210, 2030.220, 2030.230, and 2030.240. (2) The party's failure to serve a timely response was the result of mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect." (emphasis added.) Even if the court were to impute a motion for this relief into the Opposition papers - and it does not - the court does not find that Respondents' failure to serve a timely response was the result of mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect, and the court does not find that Respondents have served a late response that substantially complies with the requirements under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2030.210 [Mode of response; required contents], 2030.220 [Answers in response; contents and form; obligations of responding party], 2030.230 [Answers supplemented by attached documents; specificity of references to attachments; examination and inspection by responding party], and 2030.240 [Partial objection to form of interrogatory; response; statement of claim of privilege or work product]. For the reasons discussed above, Petitioner Chun Yen Chen's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories from Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen to respond to Defendant's Form Interrogatories 2.1-2.8, 2.11-2.13, 12.1-12.7, 14.1-14.2, 50.2, 50.5-50.6 - Set One, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedures sec. 2030.300(a), is GRANTED.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSE CHIHHSI CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Set One From Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen; Notice Of Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,185.00 Against Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 4 of 5 - Mikki Chen] Mikki Chen shall serve responses to Form Interrogatories 2.1-2.8, 2.11-2.13, 12.1-12.7, 14.1-14.2, 50.2, 50.5-50.6 - Set One, within ten (10) days of service of Notice of Entry of Order. The responses must comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.210: In the first paragraph of the response immediately below the title of the case, there shall appear the identity of the responding party, the set number, and the identity of the propounding party. Each answer in the response shall bear the same identifying number and be in the same sequence as the corresponding interrogatory. Mikki Chen shall respond in writing, under oath, and separately to each interrogatory, with an answer containing the information sought to be discovered. Per Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.220, (a) Each answer in a response to interrogatories shall be as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available to the responding party permits. (b) If an interrogatory cannot be answered completely, it shall be answered to the extent possible. (c) If the responding party does not have personal knowledge sufficient to respond fully to an interrogatory, that party shall so state, but shall make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information by inquiry to other natural persons or organizations, except where the information is equally available to the propounding party. In the Opposition and the communications between Petitioner's counsel and Andy Chen, Respondents express confusion about the meaning of the term "incident" as used in the Form Interrogatories. The term "Incident" is defined in Section 4 the Form Interrogatories: "(1) INCIDENT includes the circumstances and events surrounding the alleged accident, injury, or other occurrence or breach of contract giving rise to this action or proceeding." Petitioner's attorney echoed this definition of "incident" his February 28, 2024 email: "[T]he claim that [sic] issues you raised in this case (i.e. contract agreement, 855 La Playay [sic], #462, San Francisco, California 94121, Popeye's, stock shares)." (Decl. of Yang, Ex.10 at page 2.) Respondent Mikki Chen shall respond to the Form Interrogatories that use the term "Incident" as the term is defined in Section 4 of the Form. If Mikki Chen fails to obey this Order compelling answers, the court may make further orders that are just, including the imposition of an issue sanction, an evidence sanction, or a terminating sanction under Code of Civil procedure section 2023.010 et seq. In lieu of or in addition to that sanction, the court may impose a monetary sanction under Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.010 et seq. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290(c).)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSE CHIHHSI CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Set One From Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen; Notice Of Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,185.00 Against Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 5 of 5 - Mikki Chen] Petitioner's request that Mikki Chen be ordered to pay monetary sanctions pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.300(d) is DENIED, on the finding that other circumstances make the imposition of monetary sanction on Mikki Chen, personally, unjust. On the record before the court, Andy Chen acted on behalf of himself and as Mikki Chen's putative attorney-in-fact in failing to properly respond to the instant Form Interrogatories and in unsuccessfully opposing the instant Motion. The Opposition asserts Mikki Chen has limited English language skills and "faces significant barriers in comprehending and responding to legal documents." (Opp. at 5:4-5). Mikki Chen' personal email address was not included on the communications between counsel and Andy Chen except on a single email from Petitioner's attorney, dated February 28, 2024, after the deficient supplemental responses were served. (see, Ex. D to Opp.) There is no showing Mikki Chen separately communicated with Petitioner's attorney regarding the Form Interrogatories or this Motion. The objections and responses served in January and February 2024 were captioned "joint" responses from both Mikki Chen and Andi Chen (see, Decl. of Yang, Exs. 3 and 9). Likewise, a single "joint" Opposition was filed in response to the separately filed and noticed motions to compel responses, identifying Andy Chen as "Self-Represented Respondent with full Power of Attorney for Mikki May Chiu Chen." (Opp. at 12:16-20.) On the record before the court, there is no clear indication that Mikki Chen was aware of or endorsed Andy Chen's actions as her putative attorney-in-fact in failing to properly respond to the Form Interrogatories and unsuccessfully opposing this Motion. Andy Chen will be ordered to pay monetary sanctions pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.300(d) in a separate concurrently issued order in this matter. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSE CHIHHSI CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Set One From Respondent Andrew Chen Aka Andy Chen; Notice Of Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,185.00 Against Respondent Andrew Chen Aka Andy Chen (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 5 - Andy Chen] Self-represented parties must comply with statutory deadlines and are not immune from the consequences of a failure to do so. (Stover v. Bruntz (2017) 12 Cal.App.5th 19, 31, failure to comply with time limit for responding to requests for admissions, and imposition of statutory sanctions). Self-represented parties must be treated in the same manner as other litigants and attorneys, and must follow the correct rules of procedure, and all applicable statutes, Local Rules, and the California Rules of Court. (Rappleyea v Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 984-985; see also, Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 1.6(15), which defines "parties" as including both self-represented persons and persons represented by an attorney of record without making any distinction between them). Responses to the Form Interrogatories Petitioner served on December 4, 2023, were due January 8, 2024. Respondents did not serve responses to the Form Interrogatories by January 8, 2024. The record of communications between Petitioner's counsel and Andy Chen show that Petitioner extended the deadline for responses more than once after January 8, 2024 - ultimately to February 23, 2024 - but did not allow them to respond with objections or otherwise relieve Respondents of having waived any right to exercise the option to produce writings under Section 2030.230, as well as any objection to the interrogatories (see, Decl. of Yang, Exs. 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8; and Opp. Exs. A, B, D; see also, Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290(a)(1)). The Opposition argues, with no analysis, "It's important to note that CCCP [sic] sec 2030.290(a)(1) and 2030.290(a)(2) implicitly recognize the concept of substantial compliance as a valid defense against accusations of noncompliance." (Opp. 2:24-25.) Respondents misunderstand this portion of the statute. A party who fails to respond to interrogatories by the statutory deadline waives any right to exercise the option to produce writings under Section 2030.230, as well as any objection to the interrogatories (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290(a)(1)). Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290(a), "The court, on motion, may relieve that party from this waiver on its determination that both of the following conditions are satisfied: (1) The party has subsequently served a response that is in substantial compliance with Sections 2030.210, 2030.220, 2030.230, and 2030.240. (2) The party's failure to serve a timely response was the result of mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect." (emphasis added.) Even if the court were to impute a motion for this relief into the Opposition papers - and it does not - the court does not find that Respondents' failure to serve a timely response was the result of mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect, and the court does not find that Respondents have served a late response that substantially complies with the requirements under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2030.210 [Mode of response; required contents], 2030.220 [Answers in response; contents and form; obligations of responding party], 2030.230 [Answers supplemented by attached documents; specificity of references to attachments; examination and inspection by responding party], and 2030.240 [Partial objection to form of interrogatory; response; statement of claim of privilege or work product].

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSE CHIHHSI CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Set One From Respondent Andrew Chen Aka Andy Chen; Notice Of Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,185.00 Against Respondent Andrew Chen Aka Andy Chen (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 4 of 5 - Andy Chen] For the reasons discussed above, Petitioner Chun Yen Chen's Motion to Compel Further Responses from Respondent Andrew Chen aka Andy Chen to respond to Defendant's Form Interrogatories 2.1-2.8, 2.11-2.13, 12.1-12.7, 14.1-14.2, 50.2, 50.5-50.6 - Set One, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedures sec. 2030.300(a), is GRANTED. Andy Chen shall serve responses to Form Interrogatories 2.1-2.8, 2.11-2.13, 12.1-12.7, 14.1-14.2, 50.2, 50.5-50.6 - Set One, within ten (10) days of service if Notice of Entry of Order. The responses must comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.210: In the first paragraph of the response immediately below the title of the case, there shall appear the identity of the responding party, the set number, and the identity of the propounding party. Each answer in the response shall bear the same identifying number and be in the same sequence as the corresponding interrogatory. Andy Chen shall respond in writing, under oath, and separately to each interrogatory, with an answer containing the information sought to be discovered. Per Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.220, (a) Each answer in a response to interrogatories shall be as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available to the responding party permits. (b) If an interrogatory cannot be answered completely, it shall be answered to the extent possible. (c) If the responding party does not have personal knowledge sufficient to respond fully to an interrogatory, that party shall so state, but shall make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information by inquiry to other natural persons or organizations, except where the information is equally available to the propounding party. In the Opposition and the communications between Petitioner's counsel and Andy Chen, Respondents express confusion about the meaning of the term "incident" as used in the Form Interrogatories. The term "Incident" is defined in Section 4 the Form Interrogatories: "(1) INCIDENT includes the circumstances and events surrounding the alleged accident, injury, or other occurrence or breach of contract giving rise to this action or proceeding." Petitioner's attorney echoed this definition of "incident" his February 28, 2024 email: "[T]he claim that [sic] issues you raised in this case (i.e. contract agreement, 855 La Playay [sic], #462, San Francisco, California 94121, Popeye's, stock shares)." (Decl. of Yang, Ex.10 at page 2.) Respondent Andy Chen shall respond to the Form Interrogatories that use the term "Incident" as the term is defined in Section 4 of the Form. If Andy Chen fails to obey this Order compelling answers, the court may make further orders that are just, including the imposition of an issue sanction, an evidence sanction, or a terminating sanction under Code of Civil procedure section 2023.010 et seq. In lieu of or in addition to that sanction, the court may impose a monetary sanction under Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.010 et seq. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290(c).) Petitioner's request that Andy Chen be ordered to pay monetary sanctions pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.300(d) is GRANTED. The court does not find Respondent Andy Chen acted with substantial justification in opposing the motion to compel further responses to the Form Interrogatories or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. Andy Chen, personally and as Mikki Chen's putative attorney-in-fact, is ordered to pay monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,685.00 to Chun Yen Chen, within fifteen (15) days of service of Notice of Entry of Order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSE CHIHHSI CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Set One From Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen; Notice Of Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,185.00 Against Respondent Mikki May Chiu Chen (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 5 - Mikki Chen] Discussion: Unless otherwise limited by order of the court in accordance with this title, any party may obtain discovery regarding any matter, not privileged, that is relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action [ ], if the matter either is itself admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Discovery may relate to the claim or defense of the party seeking discovery or of any other party to the action. Discovery may be obtained of the identity and location of persons having knowledge of any discoverable matter, as well as of the existence, description, nature, custody, condition, and location of any document, electronically stored information, tangible thing, or land or other property. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010) Any party may obtain discovery by one or more of the following methods: (a) Oral and written depositions. (b) Interrogatories to a party. (c) Inspections of documents, things, and places. (d) Physical and mental examinations. (e) Requests for admissions. (f) Simultaneous exchanges of expert trial witness information. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2019.010, emphasis added.) Subject to certain statutory restrictions (Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010 et seq and Code of Civil Procedure section 2019.010 et seq), any party may obtain discovery by propounding to any other party to the action written interrogatories to be answered under oath. An interrogatory may relate to whether another party is making a certain contention, or to the facts, witnesses, and writings on which a contention is based. An interrogatory is not objectionable because an answer to it involves an opinion or contention that relates to fact or the application of law to fact, or would be based on information obtained or legal theories developed in anticipation of litigation or in preparation for trial. (Civ. Proc. Code section 2030.010) The Judicial Council, the policymaking body of the California courts, has developed and approved official Form Interrogatories (Form FI-120). Form interrogatories can be used in a civil action that is based on a breach of contract claim. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.710). On October 27, 2023, Mikki May Chiu Chen filed a petition pursuant to Probate Code section 850, alleging elder abuse and claiming an ownership interest in: A condominium at 855 La Playa, #462 San Francisco, CA 94121; 'Sweet Potato Enterprises, Inc.', owner of a Popeyes franchise located at 599 Divisadero Street, San Francisco, CA 94117; Equity in stocks: 2916 shares of Tesla and 310 shares of Apple. On November 1, 2023, CHUN YEN Chen filed a competing petition pursuant to Probate Code section 850, claiming the estate's ownership interests in the same assets, and seeking additional statutory damages, and civil and punitive damages. Both petitions bring claims based on alleged breaches of contract. Form interrogatories can be used by both petitioning parties in this matter. The party served with Form Interrogatories must serve responses within 30 days of service of the Form Interrogatories (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.260(a)). If a party fails to serve a timely response to the interrogatories, the party has waived any right to exercise the option to produce writings under Section 2030.230, as well as any objection to the interrogatories, including one based on privilege or on the protection for work product. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290(a)(1).)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT WHITE ANBIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared: (1) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (form DE-121) is on file; however, failed to include "LOST" will being offered. Prob. Code sec. 8100 and CRC 7.50. (2) Need allegation regarding the presumption of revocation. Prob. Code sec. 6124. If the presumption applicable, allegation and evidence will be required to overcome the presumption of revocation. (3) Petitioner is not named to act as the personal rep in the will (or Executor with a partial task) Proposed Administrator with Will Annexed appears to be applicable, not Executor. (4) Will does not waive bond for Petitioner. Petition seeks a bond of $25,000 to be ordered (for a non CA resident); however, the decedent's lost will does not waive the bond for the proposed personal representative, and not all named beneficiaries have waived the bond. A full bond (for I&A value) will be required. As no Final I&A has been filed, the court has no information of the literary property. (5) Need written declinations to act from the named executors. The will named King and Spilker as co-executors to perform certain tasks. (6) Proposed order to include a copy of the lost will being offered to be admitted. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL FLOERSCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/20/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Now, an amended petition has been filed. A fully amended petition requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [California Rules of Court 7.53(a).] (2) No proof of service of the Notice of Amended Petition for Letters of Administration has been filed. (3) No proof of publication of the notice of the Amended Petition for Letters of Administration has been filed. (4) The caption of the amended petition is incomplete if any IAEA authority is being requested. (5) Items 2c, 2d, and 3a of the amended petitioner are incomplete. (6) The waiver of bond is incomplete at item 4 and was not dated. (7) Item 5a(7) or (8) of the amended petition was not checked. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/10/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (There was no need to attach original certificates of death; item 8 of a petition for letters of administration should just state the dates of death or any predeceased person. There was no need to list the nephews in item 8 or to give notice to them, as their father is the heir. The Court could appoint without any IAEA authority, since neither the caption nor item 2c of the amended petition indicated any such request, but that would severely limit what the personal representative could do. You are strongly urged to hire an attorney.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PETER CAMERON QUAYLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate will be set for 4/21/2025. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 4/21/2025 if no petition has been filed.
